reason: Both correctly recommend a Postgres-backed queue and correctly frame 50k/day as trivial volume. The key differentiator is Answer B explicitly covers the underrated transactional-enqueue win (with the concrete "dual-write bug" example), gives a clear threshold for when to graduate to a dedicated broker, and correctly notes SQS's niche (AWS-native/serverless) — all points the rubric flags as important. Answer A is accurate but terse to the point of omission: it never mentions transactional enqueue at all, which is one of the most decision-relevant reasons to prefer Postgres here, and gives no guidance on when to reconsider. B's extra length is substantive (each bullet adds real decision-relevant content), not padding, so it doesn't violate the "length isn't merit" rule — it's simply more complete and equally clear.
